title: Enrollment Calls vs Discovery Calls Quiz

questions.question: What is the fundamental difference between a B2B discovery call and a creator enrollment call?

options.text: B2B calls are shorter while enrollment calls are longer.
B2B calls are logic-driven around business ROI, while enrollment calls are identity-driven around personal transformation.
B2B calls require video while enrollment calls are phone-only.
There is no meaningful difference; the same frameworks work for both.

questions.explanation: B2B discovery calls center on diagnosing business problems and demonstrating ROI to a buying committee. Creator enrollment calls center on personal aspiration, identity change, and helping someone feel safe enough to invest in their own transformation.

questions.question: According to the lesson, what is a creator's primary competition on an enrollment call?

options.text: Other coaching programs and courses in the same niche.
Free content available on YouTube and social media.
Inaction — the prospect deciding to do nothing and stay where they are.
The prospect's spouse or partner who controls the budget.

questions.explanation: Unlike B2B sales where prospects compare multiple vendors, creator enrollment prospects are primarily choosing between investing in themselves and doing nothing. Your competition is inaction, not another program.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of the five phases of an enrollment conversation outlined in the lesson?

options.text: Budget Qualification — determining how much the prospect can spend.
Aspiration — exploring where the prospect wants to be, including lifestyle and identity goals.
Competitive Analysis — positioning your offer against alternatives.
Committee Mapping — identifying all stakeholders in the decision.

questions.explanation: The five phases are Connection, Situation, Aspiration, The Gap, and The Invitation. The Aspiration phase explores where the prospect wants to be — not just in revenue terms, but in lifestyle and identity terms. This is the key element that B2B discovery frameworks completely miss.

questions.question: Think about your current sales or enrollment approach. Where do you catch yourself slipping into B2B-style selling (over-qualifying, leading with features, avoiding money conversations)? What specific change will you make on your next enrollment call based on what you learned in this lesson?
questions.aiPrompt: Evaluate whether the student identifies a specific B2B habit they default to and proposes a concrete alternative. Look for self-awareness about their current approach and a specific, actionable change rather than vague intentions like 'I will be more empathetic.' Strong answers reference specific concepts from the lesson such as the enrollment mindset shifts or the five phases.
